pending.body: verify_and_comment_mappings (planned_elsewhere.rs:247) and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs:674) compute verification outcomes but never persist the updated mapped_bead_verified=true back to the NDJSON file. This means every subsequent call to verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success will re-process the same mappings (re-verifying via br show and re-posting the same br comment on the mapped-to bead). Fix: after verify_and_comment_mappings returns, update the NDJSON entries for mappings whose verified field changed to true, or adopt an append-only design where a verification event supersedes the original unverified record.

In final_review.rs:1503, the force-completed early return checks `!final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` without distinguishing planned-elsewhere amendments. When ALL accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere and restart_count >= max_restarts (from prior rounds), this path triggers unnecessarily, setting force_completed=true and emitting a misleading 'restart cap reached' summary. The check should be `final_accepted_amendments.iter().any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none()) && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` to only force-complete when there are actual regular amendments that would require a restart.

After posting a planned-elsewhere `br comment`, run `br sync --flush-only` (or batch comments before a final sync) so the comment mutation is durable. Reconciliation syncs at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:200-209` and only then posts comments via `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:285-313`; `comment_bead` marks the adapter dirty until `sync_flush` runs (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:510-512`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:607-617`), so successful comments can be lost on exit.

Persist post-run verification results back into milestone state/journal. The engine records every mapping as `mapped_bead_verified: false` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7613-7618`), and reconciliation filters on that flag (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:699-718`), but `verify_and_comment_mappings` only returns outcomes and never updates `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` or the journal (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:247-323`). As written, a verified mapping stays permanently 'unverified', so any reconciliation replay will re-verify and re-comment the same bead.

Make the journal and `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` writes recoverable/atomic. `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` writes the journal first and says the sidecar can be rebuilt from it (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2348-2390`), but `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` only reads the sidecar (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2395-2401`) and the engine merely logs and continues on sidecar-write failure (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7621-7657`). A crash or I/O error after the journal append leaves a mapping present in audit history but invisible to later reload/comment processing (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:684-702`).

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
